title: Daily Briefing — Apr 06
headline: Extreme fear meets contracting volatility as indices pin the 20 MA — watch the flows, not the narrative.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us: SPY ($655.83) and QQQ ($584.98) are both hovering right on their daily 20 MAs — this is the battleground. The Fear & Greed index is screaming Extreme Fear at 20.9, which we know is historically an opportunity zone (see R013). VIX remains elevated at 25, but it's now trending below its SMA20, so vol is compressing despite the panic. Less emotion, more price action, please.

Sector rotation is all over the shop — Energy (XLE) and Financials (XLF) are showing improvement but in the 'elevated risk' bucket, while Materials and Utilities are rolling over. Most telling? XLF ($49.53) is still above its 20 MA, acting as the canary. If that cracks, run for cover. At the same time, beaten up sectors like Real Estate (XLRE $41.61) are triggering bullish signals. Classic bear-to-bull rotation setups, but size small while smart money's on the sidelines.

Contrarian hat firmly on: when everyone’s scared witless and retail is dumping, I start shopping, not panicking. But rules are rules — if indices daily close decisively below the 20 MA, step aside and respect price. Patience, react, don’t predict. Consensus is gloom, but if it’s in the press, it’s in the price.
callout_body:
SPY: $658.09 (SMA20) — Bulls must defend here
QQQ: $589.01 (SMA20) — Close below = risk off
XLF: $49.14 (SMA20) — Canary support zone
VIX: 25 (compressing) — Watch for break
Fear & Greed: 20.9 (Extreme Fear) — Window for rotation
XLRE: $41.37 (SMA20) — Real estate attempting a base
action_items: Tactical longs in XLRE, D, IWM — but only if daily closes hold above 20 MA.
Watch XLF like a hawk: breakdown below $49.14 triggers defensive, per Rule R009.
closing_quote: Missed the last move? Abundance mindset. Less trades = more wins.
